praxisTitle: Qwen 3.6 Plus as Hermes system-audit reviewer
learned: Hermes learned the Praxis objective: Qwen 3.6 Plus may catch Hermes/system audit issues missed by the current default model, especially when reviewing logs and tool traces with visible reasoning.
Hermes identified the smallest safe first test: Run Qwen 3.6 Plus on one Hermes/Agent Radar log bundle and ask for concrete bugs, misconfigurations, and next fixes with file/line evidence.
Hermes mapped the stop rule: Kill if it only gives generic advice, cannot cite exact evidence, or takes longer/costs more than the current model for the same audit.
Hermes found 4 source URL(s) to verify before adoption.

praxisTitle: Mobile remote-agent ops bakeoff: current Termius/tmux vs C3
learned: Hermes read Praxis: Mobile remote-agent ops bakeoff: current Termius/tmux vs C3
Hermes followed the batch objective: Study real-world agent workflows overnight, extract reusable lessons, and draft skills only from evidence-backed Praxis.
Hermes learned the reusable workflow: A dedicated mobile coding-agent IDE only matters if it beats the existing Termius+tmux workflow on visibility or handoff.
Hermes identified the smallest safe first test: Perform the same small repo inspection/fix from phone workflow using current setup; compare against C3 only if available without major setup.
Hermes mapped the execution path: Perform the same small repo inspection/fix from phone workflow using current setup; compare against C3 only if available without major setup.
Hermes mapped the verification/stop rule: Kill if it requires account/setup friction or does not beat Termius+tmux for one real fix.
Hermes found 3 evidence URL(s) to verify before skill adoption.
nextActions: Perform the same small repo inspection/fix from phone workflow using current setup; compare against C3 only if available without major setup.
Convert this pattern into a skill draft only after source evidence and verification stay strong.

praxisTitle: n8n-style multi-agent research flow for competitor updates
learned: Hermes read Praxis: n8n-style multi-agent research flow for competitor updates
Hermes followed the batch objective: Study real-world agent workflows overnight, extract reusable lessons, and draft skills only from evidence-backed Praxis.
Hermes learned the reusable workflow: A simple multi-agent research workflow can convert competitor/product monitoring into higher-quality Agent Radar actions than raw X doomscroll cards.
Hermes identified the smallest safe first test: Create a 5-source monitor that detects one product update and has Hermes write a sourced action with kill/next-step fields.
Hermes mapped the execution path: Create a 5-source monitor that detects one product update and has Hermes write a sourced action with kill/next-step fields.
Hermes mapped the verification/stop rule: Kill if it produces duplicate noise, misses source URLs, or cannot generate one useful action from 5 monitored sources in a day.
Hermes found 3 evidence URL(s) to verify before skill adoption.
nextActions: Create a 5-source monitor that detects one product update and has Hermes write a sourced action with kill/next-step fields.
